texts.orig: Patient Safety Issues
texts.text: Patient Safety Issues

texts.label: text
texts.orig: PSPO 827, 1930: Free-Text Dosage Display in Patient Instructions/SIG (Remedy 278019, 222104, 234281, 283190, 312285, 369872, 427630, 439883, 451557, 605155)
texts.text: PSPO 827, 1930: Free-Text Dosage Display in Patient Instructions/SIG (Remedy 278019, 222104, 234281, 283190, 312285, 369872, 427630, 439883, 451557, 605155)

texts.label: text
texts.orig: – Previously, when a provider entered a free-text dosage rather than picking one from the drop-down list, the text displayed in the Patient Instructions/SIG and order details in CPRS. It was assumed that this might be dangerous as the patients could misunderstand the dose they were to take—especially if the dosage contained a concentration for a medication such as insulin.
texts.text: – Previously, when a provider entered a free-text dosage rather than picking one from the drop-down list, the text displayed in the Patient Instructions/SIG and order details in CPRS. It was assumed that this might be dangerous as the patients could misunderstand the dose they were to take—especially if the dosage contained a concentration for a medication such as insulin.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Resolution: CPRS developers corrected this problem.
texts.text: Resolution: CPRS developers corrected this problem.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: PSPO 1777: Cytopathology Report that Printed with the Wrong Veterans (Remedy 417043)
texts.text: PSPO 1777: Cytopathology Report that Printed with the Wrong Veterans (Remedy 417043)

texts.label: text
texts.orig: – Previously, if the user was on a patient’s record and had viewed a report and then processed an alert for a different veterans results and then tried to print the Most Recent report, CPRS printed the report incorrectly. The information was for the second patient while the header was for the first patient.
texts.text: – Previously, if the user was on a patient’s record and had viewed a report and then processed an alert for a different veterans results and then tried to print the Most Recent report, CPRS printed the report incorrectly. The information was for the second patient while the header was for the first patient.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Resolution: CPRS developers disabled printing from the Most Recent report, which should not have been allowed.
texts.text: Resolution: CPRS developers disabled printing from the Most Recent report, which should not have been allowed.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: PSPO 2025: MHTC for Same Button_Associate Provider Decision
texts.text: PSPO 2025: MHTC for Same Button_Associate Provider Decision

texts.label: text
texts.orig: – When the workgroup reviewed the Primary Care button, it was decided that the Mental Health Treatment Coordinator and the Associate Provider should be on the button. But it was also felt that the location of the Associate Provider on the button could be confusing to providers and they might misinterpret what it meant.
texts.text: – When the workgroup reviewed the Primary Care button, it was decided that the Mental Health Treatment Coordinator and the Associate Provider should be on the button. But it was also felt that the location of the Associate Provider on the button could be confusing to providers and they might misinterpret what it meant.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Resolution: Following the recommendation from the workgroup, CPRS developers changed the layout of the information on the button to be clearer, as follows:
texts.text: Resolution: Following the recommendation from the workgroup, CPRS developers changed the layout of the information on the button to be clearer, as follows:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Top line: Primary Care Team / Primary Care Provider / Associate Primary Care Provider (if assigned)
texts.text: Top line: Primary Care Team / Primary Care Provider / Associate Primary Care Provider (if assigned)

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Second line (if patient is an inpatient): Inpatient Attending / Inpatient Provider
texts.text: Second line (if patient is an inpatient): Inpatient Attending / Inpatient Provider

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Third line: Mental Health Treatment Coordinator (if assigned)
texts.text: Third line: Mental Health Treatment Coordinator (if assigned)

texts.label: text
texts.orig: PSPO 2053: Free-Text Results "Collide" When Graphing Multiple Lab Tests (Remedy 499883)
texts.text: PSPO 2053: Free-Text Results "Collide" When Graphing Multiple Lab Tests (Remedy 499883)

texts.label: text
texts.orig: – Previously under a specific set of circumstances, it was possible for the lab test free-text result from one lab to incorrectly be displayed as the result in another lab. The following conditions were necessary to produce this error:
texts.text: – Previously under a specific set of circumstances, it was possible for the lab test free-text result from one lab to incorrectly be displayed as the result in another lab. The following conditions were necessary to produce this error:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Multiple lab tests must be selected.
texts.text: Multiple lab tests must be selected.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: At least two of these lab tests must have values that are free-text.
texts.text: At least two of these lab tests must have values that are free-text.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The values must be different.
texts.text: The values must be different.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The graphs must be separate (Individual Graphs checked).
texts.text: The graphs must be separate (Individual Graphs checked).

texts.label: text
texts.orig: One of the lab tests with a free-text value must be the very top item in the Items list.
texts.text: One of the lab tests with a free-text value must be the very top item in the Items list.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Resolution: Developers corrected this issue. The entries should now read correctly.
texts.text: Resolution: Developers corrected this issue. The entries should now read correctly.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: PSPO 2087: Another Person Editing Orders (Remedy 516561)
texts.text: PSPO 2087: Another Person Editing Orders (Remedy 516561)

texts.label: text
texts.orig: – In CPRS 28, when a user entered an allergy from the Orders tab, CPRS locked ordering for the selected patient until the entire CPRS session was closed—even if the user changed patients in the original session, the lock remained until the session was completely closed. A user in a different session could not order anything on the locked patient until the first session was closed. This problem did not occur if the allergy was entered from the Cover Sheet, only from the Orders tab.
texts.text: – In CPRS 28, when a user entered an allergy from the Orders tab, CPRS locked ordering for the selected patient until the entire CPRS session was closed—even if the user changed patients in the original session, the lock remained until the session was completely closed. A user in a different session could not order anything on the locked patient until the first session was closed. This problem did not occur if the allergy was entered from the Cover Sheet, only from the Orders tab.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Resolution: CPRS developers corrected this problem. The lock should be released as soon as the provider is done placing orders.
texts.text: Resolution: CPRS developers corrected this problem. The lock should be released as soon as the provider is done placing orders.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: PSPO 2152: CPRS Allergy Issue - Observed entries set to default value MODERATE (Remedy 581482) –
texts.text: PSPO 2152: CPRS Allergy Issue - Observed entries set to default value MODERATE (Remedy 581482) –

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Previously, CPRS Observed allergies defaulted to the Severity of Moderate. Because users could accept the default without making a conscious decision about severity, there could be a potential risk of using a medication that is listed with an existing Adverse Reaction (ADR)/Allergy, but with an unexpected level of reaction. This could delay treatment of lifesaving medication because the severity is rated higher than it should be, or it could cause the clinician to give a medication that causes a severe reaction when only a moderate reaction was expected.
texts.text: Previously, CPRS Observed allergies defaulted to the Severity of Moderate. Because users could accept the default without making a conscious decision about severity, there could be a potential risk of using a medication that is listed with an existing Adverse Reaction (ADR)/Allergy, but with an unexpected level of reaction. This could delay treatment of lifesaving medication because the severity is rated higher than it should be, or it could cause the clinician to give a medication that causes a severe reaction when only a moderate reaction was expected.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Resolution:  Developers changed the default for all observed ADR/Allergy entries to be BLANK (NULL), and CPRS now requires the user to select an ADR severity (Mild/Moderate/Severe).
texts.text: Resolution:  Developers changed the default for all observed ADR/Allergy entries to be BLANK (NULL), and CPRS now requires the user to select an ADR severity (Mild/Moderate/Severe).

texts.label: text
texts.orig: PSPO 2318: Order Check in CPRS after Signature from Second Monitor Displays on Primary Monitor instead of Second Monitor (Remedy 752717)
texts.text: PSPO 2318: Order Check in CPRS after Signature from Second Monitor Displays on Primary Monitor instead of Second Monitor (Remedy 752717)

texts.label: text
texts.orig: – On systems using two monitors to display applications, there was a problem where the signature form and order check dialog displayed correctly on the primary monitor, but on the secondary monitor, the order check dialog did not display.
texts.text: – On systems using two monitors to display applications, there was a problem where the signature form and order check dialog displayed correctly on the primary monitor, but on the secondary monitor, the order check dialog did not display.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Resolution: Developers corrected this problem and any affected dialogs should display correctly on the primary and secondary monitors.
texts.text: Resolution: Developers corrected this problem and any affected dialogs should display correctly on the primary and secondary monitors.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Known Issues
texts.text: Known Issues

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Cleveland Reports Discontinuing Existing Orders Creates Duplicate Order with Right-click Sign Option in CPRS Orders Tab (CQ 21308)
texts.text: Cleveland Reports Discontinuing Existing Orders Creates Duplicate Order with Right-click Sign Option in CPRS Orders Tab (CQ 21308)

texts.label: text
texts.orig: – A provider stated she noticed when discontinuing existing orders (i.e., meds, labs) the discontinue order view is duplicated. This anomaly occurs when the user highlights the unsigned discontinued order and uses the right mouse click feature to sign or uses the Sign selected… menu option from the Action menu on the toolbar. It does not occur upon selecting a new patient or refreshing patient information.
texts.text: – A provider stated she noticed when discontinuing existing orders (i.e., meds, labs) the discontinue order view is duplicated. This anomaly occurs when the user highlights the unsigned discontinued order and uses the right mouse click feature to sign or uses the Sign selected… menu option from the Action menu on the toolbar. It does not occur upon selecting a new patient or refreshing patient information.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Workaround:
texts.text: Workaround:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Clinicians continue with discontinue process. Order does discontinue.
texts.text: Clinicians continue with discontinue process. Order does discontinue.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Comments:
texts.text: Comments:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The provider stated it was by no means aggravating to see duplicate orders but believed it was important to report.
texts.text: The provider stated it was by no means aggravating to see duplicate orders but believed it was important to report.

texts.text: Kansas City and Wichita reported that the CPRS Labs tab does not respect the CPRS font display setting. The CPRS font display was set to size 12 and the details of the lab were displayed at 8. When changing the font size in CPRS the lab tab information does not respect the font.

texts.orig: Cleveland Reports that View Icon Legend Displays Error Message (CQ 21241)
texts.text: Cleveland Reports that View Icon Legend Displays Error Message (CQ 21241)

texts.label: text
texts.orig: – Cleveland reported that when trying to view the Icon Legend from the View menu of the Notes tab, CPRS displayed the following error message: “Error reading fraImgText.BorderStyle: Property BorderStyle does not exist.” After some research, it was discovered that this error message also occurs on the Consults, Surgery, and D/C Summary tabs as well.
texts.text: – Cleveland reported that when trying to view the Icon Legend from the View menu of the Notes tab, CPRS displayed the following error message: “Error reading fraImgText.BorderStyle: Property BorderStyle does not exist.” After some research, it was discovered that this error message also occurs on the Consults, Surgery, and D/C Summary tabs as well.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Users Can Enter and Order a Schedule 1 Controlled Substance (CQ21405)
texts.text: Users Can Enter and Order a Schedule 1 Controlled Substance (CQ21405)

texts.label: text
texts.orig: –Customer Product Support was able to enter, via CPRS and digitally sign a Schedule 1 controlled substance. In addition, they were able to use Outpatient Pharmacy to finish and dispense the medication. Schedule 1 includes investigational controlled substances and illegal controlled substances. EPCS regulations for PRESCRIBING do not allow an electronic Rx for a Schedule I product.
texts.text: –Customer Product Support was able to enter, via CPRS and digitally sign a Schedule 1 controlled substance. In addition, they were able to use Outpatient Pharmacy to finish and dispense the medication. Schedule 1 includes investigational controlled substances and illegal controlled substances. EPCS regulations for PRESCRIBING do not allow an electronic Rx for a Schedule I product.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Workaround:
texts.text: Workaround:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: None
texts.text: None

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Comments:
texts.text: Comments:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Due to the low impact and severity of this issue, it will be resolved in CPRS v30. Documents reflect Schedule 2-5 drugs can be utilized with ePCS. In addition, Per DEA, there are no Schedule 1 drugs that can be prescribed electronically or via paper unless they are strictly study drugs.
texts.text: Due to the low impact and severity of this issue, it will be resolved in CPRS v30. Documents reflect Schedule 2-5 drugs can be utilized with ePCS. In addition, Per DEA, there are no Schedule 1 drugs that can be prescribed electronically or via paper unless they are strictly study drugs.

texts.orig: New Parameters
texts.text: New Parameters

texts.label: text
texts.orig: OR DEA PIV LINK MSG
texts.text: OR DEA PIV LINK MSG

texts.label: text
texts.orig: : This parameter enables sites to customize the text that displays on a dialog when the user is not allowed to place orders for controlled substances, but they believe that they should be allowed. The site can put text into this parameter to display to the user.
texts.text: : This parameter enables sites to customize the text that displays on a dialog when the user is not allowed to place orders for controlled substances, but they believe that they should be allowed. The site can put text into this parameter to display to the user.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: ORCDGMRC EARLIEST DATE DEFAULT:
texts.text: ORCDGMRC EARLIEST DATE DEFAULT:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: At the request of test sites, this parameter was created to enable sites to set a default date for the Earliest Available Date field in the consult request dialog. The default can be left blank to force the provider to answer, set to today, or set as a future relative amount of time (T+30). When OR*3.0*306 is installed, a post installation routine will set the default to TODAY. Sites can delete the value or set a new one using XPAR MENU TOOLS.
texts.text: At the request of test sites, this parameter was created to enable sites to set a default date for the Earliest Available Date field in the consult request dialog. The default can be left blank to force the provider to answer, set to today, or set as a future relative amount of time (T+30). When OR*3.0*306 is installed, a post installation routine will set the default to TODAY. Sites can delete the value or set a new one using XPAR MENU TOOLS.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Note:
texts.text: Note:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The post install routine does NOT change the default Earliest Available Date value in quick orders. It does check which quick orders have a value defined and send a list of those quick orders to the person who installs patch OR*3.0*306. The installer must then forward the MailMan message to the person who can check the value in the quick order and delete the value defined there to make it use what is in the parameter, leave the value as defined in the quick order, or change the value in the quick order.
texts.text: The post install routine does NOT change the default Earliest Available Date value in quick orders. It does check which quick orders have a value defined and send a list of those quick orders to the person who installs patch OR*3.0*306. The installer must then forward the MailMan message to the person who can check the value in the quick order and delete the value defined there to make it use what is in the parameter, leave the value as defined in the quick order, or change the value in the quick order.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: ORQQPL SUPPRESS CODES:
texts.text: ORQQPL SUPPRESS CODES:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The purpose of this parameter is to hide the SNOMED and ICD-9-CM codes from displaying in CPRS when defining a problem. If the CPRS coordinator selects YES, the codes will not display when searching for a term.
texts.text: The purpose of this parameter is to hide the SNOMED and ICD-9-CM codes from displaying in CPRS when defining a problem. If the CPRS coordinator selects YES, the codes will not display when searching for a term.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: New Functionality
texts.text: New Functionality

texts.label: text
texts.orig: NSR 20070510, NSR 20070525: SNOMED CT Conversion for the Problems Tab
texts.text: NSR 20070510, NSR 20070525: SNOMED CT Conversion for the Problems Tab

texts.label: text
texts.orig: To help clinicians locate the correct term to identify the patient’s condition, CPRS is now using SNOMED Concept Terms (SNOMED CT) on the Problem List and Encounter form. SNOMED CT is a classification system that uses terms originally created by the College of American Pathologists (CAP), so it was created by physicians for physicians. Using SNOMED CT should make it easier for clinicians to identify the correct term rather than having to search through ICD codes, which function as billing codes for insurance purposes.
texts.text: To help clinicians locate the correct term to identify the patient’s condition, CPRS is now using SNOMED Concept Terms (SNOMED CT) on the Problem List and Encounter form. SNOMED CT is a classification system that uses terms originally created by the College of American Pathologists (CAP), so it was created by physicians for physicians. Using SNOMED CT should make it easier for clinicians to identify the correct term rather than having to search through ICD codes, which function as billing codes for insurance purposes.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: While this change should make it easier for clinicians to find the problems they want, it will take some adjustments on the part of clinicians. If clinicians normally enter an ICD-9-CM code, they will no longer find a code using this method. Clinicians need to enter a term to search for.
texts.text: While this change should make it easier for clinicians to find the problems they want, it will take some adjustments on the part of clinicians. If clinicians normally enter an ICD-9-CM code, they will no longer find a code using this method. Clinicians need to enter a term to search for.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The new search is found on the Problem List Lexicon Search dialog and the Lookup Diagnosis dialog on the Encounter form and anywhere else where the same lookup dialogs are used, such as for the provisional diagnosis on the Consults tab.
texts.text: The new search is found on the Problem List Lexicon Search dialog and the Lookup Diagnosis dialog on the Encounter form and anywhere else where the same lookup dialogs are used, such as for the provisional diagnosis on the Consults tab.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Problems Tab Changes
texts.text: Problems Tab Changes

texts.label: text
texts.orig: On the Problems tab, there can be up to a three-tiered process to identify a term that most closely identifies the patient’s condition.
texts.text: On the Problems tab, there can be up to a three-tiered process to identify a term that most closely identifies the patient’s condition.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Initially, the user can search for the patient’s problem by entering a term to bring up a selection of SNOMED CT terms.
texts.text: Initially, the user can search for the patient’s problem by entering a term to bring up a selection of SNOMED CT terms.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: If users do not find the item in the results from the first search, CPRS has added an Extend Search button. The Extend Search button goes to the SNOMED clinical hierarchy to find additional terms, including terms that may not currently be mapped to ICD-9-CM codes (799.9 codes).
texts.text: If users do not find the item in the results from the first search, CPRS has added an Extend Search button. The Extend Search button goes to the SNOMED clinical hierarchy to find additional terms, including terms that may not currently be mapped to ICD-9-CM codes (799.9 codes).

texts.label: text
texts.orig: If a sufficiently descriptive term is still not found, users can also type in a free-text entry. Coordinators will have reports to track free-text entries and ensure that they get resolved.
texts.text: If a sufficiently descriptive term is still not found, users can also type in a free-text entry. Coordinators will have reports to track free-text entries and ensure that they get resolved.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: However, even after extending the search, the item may not be found and a request for a new term may be needed.
texts.text: However, even after extending the search, the item may not be found and a request for a new term may be needed.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Two bulletins were created to one, inform coordinators that a new term was requested, and two, to let them know if a code is selected for a code that is not mapped to an appropriate ICD-9-CM code, but has a 799.9 code instead.
texts.text: Two bulletins were created to one, inform coordinators that a new term was requested, and two, to let them know if a code is selected for a code that is not mapped to an appropriate ICD-9-CM code, but has a 799.9 code instead.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: 799.9 Codes
texts.text: 799.9 Codes

texts.label: text
texts.orig: It is possible to select a code that maps to a 799 ICD-9-CM code 9 (Other unknown and unspecified cause of morbidity or mortality). If the user selects a term that is not mapped to a code and would need a 799.9 code, a dialog warns the user that the code will not be available for selection on the Encounter form and that it will be sent for review to see if the selected term should be mapped. The user can then either select the term or choose to cancel the selection and begin searching again.
texts.text: It is possible to select a code that maps to a 799 ICD-9-CM code 9 (Other unknown and unspecified cause of morbidity or mortality). If the user selects a term that is not mapped to a code and would need a 799.9 code, a dialog warns the user that the code will not be available for selection on the Encounter form and that it will be sent for review to see if the selected term should be mapped. The user can then either select the term or choose to cancel the selection and begin searching again.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: If the user selects the 799.9 term, CPRS sends a mapping request to have the term mapped to an appropriate ICD-9-CM code. When the code is appropriately mapped, the problem will automatically be updated to the new correct code in CPRS.
texts.text: If the user selects the 799.9 term, CPRS sends a mapping request to have the term mapped to an appropriate ICD-9-CM code. When the code is appropriately mapped, the problem will automatically be updated to the new correct code in CPRS.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Terms Not Defined as SNOMED Terms
texts.text: Terms Not Defined as SNOMED Terms

texts.label: text
texts.orig: A user might enter as term that does not map to a SNOMED CT term in the Problem List subset. If CPRS searches for the term and does not find it, the Extend Search button is available, which extends the search to the SNOMED CT clinical findings hierarchy. If the user extends the search and no term is found again, CPRS displays a dialog informing the user that no term was found. The user can then choose to redo the search by entering a new search term or to request that a new SNOMED term be created for what the user entered.
texts.text: A user might enter as term that does not map to a SNOMED CT term in the Problem List subset. If CPRS searches for the term and does not find it, the Extend Search button is available, which extends the search to the SNOMED CT clinical findings hierarchy. If the user extends the search and no term is found again, CPRS displays a dialog informing the user that no term was found. The user can then choose to redo the search by entering a new search term or to request that a new SNOMED term be created for what the user entered.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: If the user chooses to request a new term, a request bulletin will be sent to the site’s local review team. If that team concurs that a new term should be created, they can initiate an NTRT request to add a new SNOMED term to the subset based on what the user entered. If a new SNOMED term is created, it will NOT automatically update when the term is created.
texts.text: If the user chooses to request a new term, a request bulletin will be sent to the site’s local review team. If that team concurs that a new term should be created, they can initiate an NTRT request to add a new SNOMED term to the subset based on what the user entered. If a new SNOMED term is created, it will NOT automatically update when the term is created.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Encounter Forms Changes
texts.text: Encounter Forms Changes

texts.label: text
texts.orig: When the user selects the Other Diagnosis… button on the Encounter form’s Diagnosis tab, the Lookup Diagnosis dialog searches for problems using what the user has entered to find the SNOMED terms. However, the Encounter form diagnoses must have an associated ICD code. Currently, the code comes from the ICD-9-CM clinical subset of terms. If the original search does not find the term, the Lookup Diagnosis dialog also has an Extend Search button. Because it must have an ICD-9-CM code, the extended search on this dialog goes to the ICD-9-CM subset of terms. However all codes returned from the extended search will have an associated code—even if that code is a 799.9 code that is unmapped.
texts.text: When the user selects the Other Diagnosis… button on the Encounter form’s Diagnosis tab, the Lookup Diagnosis dialog searches for problems using what the user has entered to find the SNOMED terms. However, the Encounter form diagnoses must have an associated ICD code. Currently, the code comes from the ICD-9-CM clinical subset of terms. If the original search does not find the term, the Lookup Diagnosis dialog also has an Extend Search button. Because it must have an ICD-9-CM code, the extended search on this dialog goes to the ICD-9-CM subset of terms. However all codes returned from the extended search will have an associated code—even if that code is a 799.9 code that is unmapped.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Consults and Procedures
texts.text: Consults and Procedures

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Because some sites can set their Consults and Procedures to require a provisional diagnosis from the Lexicon search, the SNOMED codes can also be seen on the Other Diagnosis search on these tabs as well. As with the Encounter form in current CPRS, mapping to a code means that the consult or procedure must have an ICD-9-CM code. You cannot request a New SNOMED term from the consults and procedures.
texts.text: Because some sites can set their Consults and Procedures to require a provisional diagnosis from the Lexicon search, the SNOMED codes can also be seen on the Other Diagnosis search on these tabs as well. As with the Encounter form in current CPRS, mapping to a code means that the consult or procedure must have an ICD-9-CM code. You cannot request a New SNOMED term from the consults and procedures.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: New Parameter to Show or Hide Codes
texts.text: New Parameter to Show or Hide Codes

texts.label: text
texts.orig: With these changes, developers also added a new parameter that will suppress the display of the SNOMED and ICD codes: ORQQPL SUPPRESS CODES. When the user sets this parameter to Yes, CPRS does not display SNOMED, ICD, and even CPT codes.
texts.text: With these changes, developers also added a new parameter that will suppress the display of the SNOMED and ICD codes: ORQQPL SUPPRESS CODES. When the user sets this parameter to Yes, CPRS does not display SNOMED, ICD, and even CPT codes.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Reporting Capabilities
texts.text: Reporting Capabilities

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Two reports were created in the VistA Problem List package to track New Term Rapid Turnaround (NTRT) requests and 799.9 entries for problems and Encounter form data.
texts.text: Two reports were created in the VistA Problem List package to track New Term Rapid Turnaround (NTRT) requests and 799.9 entries for problems and Encounter form data.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Problem List NTRT Follow-Up Report (GMPL NTRT F/U RPT)
texts.text: Problem List NTRT Follow-Up Report (GMPL NTRT F/U RPT)

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Problem List Freetext Follow-Up Report (GMPL FREETEXT F/U REPORT)
texts.text: Problem List Freetext Follow-Up Report (GMPL FREETEXT F/U REPORT)

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Mental Health Treatment Coordinator on the Primary Care Team Button
texts.text: Mental Health Treatment Coordinator on the Primary Care Team Button

texts.label: text
texts.orig: CPRS now displays the Mental Health Treatment Coordinator (MHTC) on the Primary Care Team button. If assigned in Primary Care Management Module (PCMM), the MHTC will now display on the Primary Care Team button, in the detailed display when the button is selected, and on the patient demographic information under the Primary Care Team information and also listed under Mental Health Treatment Coordinator.
texts.text: CPRS now displays the Mental Health Treatment Coordinator (MHTC) on the Primary Care Team button. If assigned in Primary Care Management Module (PCMM), the MHTC will now display on the Primary Care Team button, in the detailed display when the button is selected, and on the patient demographic information under the Primary Care Team information and also listed under Mental Health Treatment Coordinator.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The location of the MHTC may be slightly different based on what other roles are assigned in PCMM. For example, because Associate Provider is only for inpatients, there should be a difference between inpatients and outpatients. For inpatients, if all roles are assigned in PCMM, the MHTC is on the third line of the Primary Care Team button, but if the patient is an outpatient, the MHTC will probably display on the second row of the Primary Care Team button.
texts.text: The location of the MHTC may be slightly different based on what other roles are assigned in PCMM. For example, because Associate Provider is only for inpatients, there should be a difference between inpatients and outpatients. For inpatients, if all roles are assigned in PCMM, the MHTC is on the third line of the Primary Care Team button, but if the patient is an outpatient, the MHTC will probably display on the second row of the Primary Care Team button.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: DEA ePrescription for Outpatient Controlled Substance Medications
texts.text: DEA ePrescription for Outpatient Controlled Substance Medications

texts.label: text
texts.orig: To comply with Drug Enforcement Agency (DEA) policy on electronic medication ordering, CPRS now requires a provider ordering outpatient controlled substances to use his or her Personal Identification Verification (PIV) card that contains a certificate that must be verified when attempting to sign the order for the medication. The PIV card is obtained at your site and the certificate should be installed by someone at your site also.
texts.text: To comply with Drug Enforcement Agency (DEA) policy on electronic medication ordering, CPRS now requires a provider ordering outpatient controlled substances to use his or her Personal Identification Verification (PIV) card that contains a certificate that must be verified when attempting to sign the order for the medication. The PIV card is obtained at your site and the certificate should be installed by someone at your site also.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Developers changed the various signature dialogs in CPRS to separate the outpatient controlled substances from the other medications ordered. When outpatient controlled substance orders need to be signed, the dialog will also have the name and DEA number of the provider signing the orders.
texts.text: Developers changed the various signature dialogs in CPRS to separate the outpatient controlled substances from the other medications ordered. When outpatient controlled substance orders need to be signed, the dialog will also have the name and DEA number of the provider signing the orders.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: When providers sign the outpatient controlled substance orders, they are agreeing to the following notice:
texts.text: When providers sign the outpatient controlled substance orders, they are agreeing to the following notice:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: By completing the two-factor authentication protocol at this time, you are legally signing the prescription(s) and authorizing the transmission of the above information to the pharmacy for dispensing. The two-factor authentication protocol may only be completed by the practitioner whose name and DEA registration number appear above.
texts.text: By completing the two-factor authentication protocol at this time, you are legally signing the prescription(s) and authorizing the transmission of the above information to the pharmacy for dispensing. The two-factor authentication protocol may only be completed by the practitioner whose name and DEA registration number appear above.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The two-factor authentication refers to the use of the PIV card, certificate, and personal identification number (PIN) when signing an outpatient controlled substance order.
texts.text: The two-factor authentication refers to the use of the PIV card, certificate, and personal identification number (PIN) when signing an outpatient controlled substance order.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: For electronically ordered outpatient controlled substances, CPRS will require the user to individually select each item to sign by checking the boxes in front of the orders. Also, above the controlled substances list are the words “Smart Card Required” to tell the user that they need a PIV card to sign the orders.
texts.text: For electronically ordered outpatient controlled substances, CPRS will require the user to individually select each item to sign by checking the boxes in front of the orders. Also, above the controlled substances list are the words “Smart Card Required” to tell the user that they need a PIV card to sign the orders.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: With the new implementation in CPRS, sites can specify for which pharmacy controlled substance schedules the provider can write orders.
texts.text: With the new implementation in CPRS, sites can specify for which pharmacy controlled substance schedules the provider can write orders.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Defects Fixes
texts.text: Defects Fixes

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Problem List - SC vs. NSC vs. Unknown (Remedy 69944)
texts.text: Problem List - SC vs. NSC vs. Unknown (Remedy 69944)

texts.label: text
texts.orig: – There was an inconsistency between the designation of service connection and treatment factors on the Problems Tab and the signature dialogs. On the Problems tab, the user was presented with a single check box to indicate Yes or No. If left blank, CPRS interpreted the blank as a No. On the signature dialogs, however, the same information had two check boxes, one for Yes and one for No. If neither was checked, CPRS interpreted this as Unknown.
texts.text: – There was an inconsistency between the designation of service connection and treatment factors on the Problems Tab and the signature dialogs. On the Problems tab, the user was presented with a single check box to indicate Yes or No. If left blank, CPRS interpreted the blank as a No. On the signature dialogs, however, the same information had two check boxes, one for Yes and one for No. If neither was checked, CPRS interpreted this as Unknown.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Resolution: CPRS developers changed the Problems tab so that it now has two check boxes for Yes and No and the system will now accept a blank entry for both as Unknown.
texts.text: Resolution: CPRS developers changed the Problems tab so that it now has two check boxes for Yes and No and the system will now accept a blank entry for both as Unknown.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Details of Old Orders (Remedy 70892)
texts.text: Details of Old Orders (Remedy 70892)

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Resolution: CPRS developers corrected this problem.
texts.text: Resolution: CPRS developers corrected this problem.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Problem Field Not Being Saved when Problem Entered in PCE Encounter Form (Remedy 130522)
texts.text: Problem Field Not Being Saved when Problem Entered in PCE Encounter Form (Remedy 130522)

texts.label: text
texts.orig: – Previously, when a user selected a diagnosis on the Encounter form’s Diagnosis tab and checked the box to add it to the Problem List, it appeared to add correctly in CPRS, but when checked in VistA, it did not save in the PROBLEM file.
texts.text: – Previously, when a user selected a diagnosis on the Encounter form’s Diagnosis tab and checked the box to add it to the Problem List, it appeared to add correctly in CPRS, but when checked in VistA, it did not save in the PROBLEM file.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Resolution: CPRS developers corrected this problem. The diagnosis saves in CPRS and to the PROBLEM file.
texts.text: Resolution: CPRS developers corrected this problem. The diagnosis saves in CPRS and to the PROBLEM file.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: ORWCH BOUNDS Question (Remedy 266396)
texts.text: ORWCH BOUNDS Question (Remedy 266396)

texts.label: text
texts.orig: – Previously, some dialogs were not saving their position nor size in the ORWCH BOUNDS parameter as they should. Therefore, when changing the size of a dialog and them closing the session and reopening, the dialog returned to its default size.
texts.text: – Previously, some dialogs were not saving their position nor size in the ORWCH BOUNDS parameter as they should. Therefore, when changing the size of a dialog and them closing the session and reopening, the dialog returned to its default size.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Resolution: CPRS developers corrected the problem and the dialogs should appropriately retain their size from session to session in CPRS.
texts.text: Resolution: CPRS developers corrected the problem and the dialogs should appropriately retain their size from session to session in CPRS.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Quick Order Quantity Quandary with Decimal Point (Remedy 276744)
texts.text: Quick Order Quantity Quandary with Decimal Point (Remedy 276744)

texts.label: text
texts.orig: – When the user entered a quick order with a fractional value and Auto-verify was set to YES, CPRS gave an Invalid Integer Error.
texts.text: – When the user entered a quick order with a fractional value and Auto-verify was set to YES, CPRS gave an Invalid Integer Error.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Resolution: CPRS developers corrected this problem and fractional values can be entered.
texts.text: Resolution: CPRS developers corrected this problem and fractional values can be entered.

texts.label: text

texts.text: Cannot Do Chart Check on DC'd Entered in Error Orders (Remedy 325209)

texts.orig: – Previously, CPRS would allow verification of discontinued orders, but it did now allow 24-hour chart check.
texts.text: – Previously, CPRS would allow verification of discontinued orders, but it did now allow 24-hour chart check.

texts.orig: Resolution: CPRS developers corrected this problem.
texts.text: Resolution: CPRS developers corrected this problem.

texts.text: Rx Patient Status Field Works Erratically (Remedy 328643)

texts.text: Resolution: CPRS developers made some corrections to the file to make it function correctly.

texts.orig: Resolution: CPRS developers corrected this problem.
texts.text: Resolution: CPRS developers corrected this problem.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Complex Dose Different than Single Dose (Remedy 385673)
texts.text: Complex Dose Different than Single Dose (Remedy 385673)

texts.label: text
texts.orig: – Previously, the dosage for a simple versus complex order for the same medication could results in getting a different dosage. For example, the simple dose might be a 40 mg tablet, which the complex might use an 80 mg tablet and use half-tablets for part of the complex dose.
texts.text: – Previously, the dosage for a simple versus complex order for the same medication could results in getting a different dosage. For example, the simple dose might be a 40 mg tablet, which the complex might use an 80 mg tablet and use half-tablets for part of the complex dose.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Resolution: Developers corrected this problem. If the single tablet is better, that dose will be used.
texts.text: Resolution: Developers corrected this problem. If the single tablet is better, that dose will be used.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Error Local Dosage Field Translation (Remedy 396719)
texts.text: Error Local Dosage Field Translation (Remedy 396719)

texts.label: text
texts.orig: – Previously, the translation for free-text dosages was not working correctly. Although the title of the Remedy ticket says “Local Dosage”, the problem was with free-text dosages.
texts.text: – Previously, the translation for free-text dosages was not working correctly. Although the title of the Remedy ticket says “Local Dosage”, the problem was with free-text dosages.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Resolution: CPRS developers changed the translation so that instead of 1.2 being put in the instructions as “1.2 tablets”, it will now read “one and 0.2 tablets”. This is the correct translation.
texts.text: Resolution: CPRS developers changed the translation so that instead of 1.2 being put in the instructions as “1.2 tablets”, it will now read “one and 0.2 tablets”. This is the correct translation.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Double-Clicking an Order via the Order Menu Will Create Two Order Dialogs (Remedy 429709)
texts.text: Double-Clicking an Order via the Order Menu Will Create Two Order Dialogs (Remedy 429709)

texts.label: text
texts.orig: – Previously, rapidly double-clicking on an order menu item could bring up multiple dialogs. In the reported case, double-clicking on an inpatient medications item instead brought up two outpatient medication dialogs.
texts.text: – Previously, rapidly double-clicking on an order menu item could bring up multiple dialogs. In the reported case, double-clicking on an inpatient medications item instead brought up two outpatient medication dialogs.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Resolution: CPRS developers corrected this problem.
texts.text: Resolution: CPRS developers corrected this problem.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Wrong Location Dialog Box Displays when a Provider Tries to Renew (Remedy 437940)
texts.text: Wrong Location Dialog Box Displays when a Provider Tries to Renew (Remedy 437940)

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Resolution: CPRS developers corrected this problem. The correct dialog now displays.
texts.text: Resolution: CPRS developers corrected this problem. The correct dialog now displays.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: User Is Being Prompted to Sign DC'd Order that Was Cancelled (Remedy 444275)
texts.text: User Is Being Prompted to Sign DC'd Order that Was Cancelled (Remedy 444275)

texts.label: text
texts.orig: – Previously, cancelling orders caused problems with both order checks and the signature dialog.
texts.text: – Previously, cancelling orders caused problems with both order checks and the signature dialog.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Resolution: CPRS developers corrected this problem. If you cancel an order on the signature dialog the order checks will update and the order will no longer display again to be signed on the signature dialog after it has been cancelled.
texts.text: Resolution: CPRS developers corrected this problem. If you cancel an order on the signature dialog the order checks will update and the order will no longer display again to be signed on the signature dialog after it has been cancelled.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Cannot Focus a Disabled or Invisible Window Error Msg Appears when Clicking on an Appt on the Cover Sheet (Remedy 478780)
texts.text: Cannot Focus a Disabled or Invisible Window Error Msg Appears when Clicking on an Appt on the Cover Sheet (Remedy 478780)

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Resolution: CPRS developers corrected this problem.
texts.text: Resolution: CPRS developers corrected this problem.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Force CPRS to Always Use MH DLL to Resolve Reminders (Remedy 483700) –
texts.text: Force CPRS to Always Use MH DLL to Resolve Reminders (Remedy 483700) –

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Previously, sites could set this parameter to either use or not use the Mental Health Dynamic Link Library (MH DLL) to process reminders.
texts.text: Previously, sites could set this parameter to either use or not use the Mental Health Dynamic Link Library (MH DLL) to process reminders.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Resolution: CPRS developers have removed the OR USE MH DLL parameter and ORQQPXRM MHDLLDMS remote procedure call.  CPRS will now only use the MH DLLs when processing a mental health test in a reminder dialog.  If the DLLs are not available, the mental health test cannot be completed.
texts.text: Resolution: CPRS developers have removed the OR USE MH DLL parameter and ORQQPXRM MHDLLDMS remote procedure call.  CPRS will now only use the MH DLLs when processing a mental health test in a reminder dialog.  If the DLLs are not available, the mental health test cannot be completed.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: RDI Currently Uses a Truncated, 10-Digit Version of the ICN in Its Calls to HDR (Remedy 486375)
texts.text: RDI Currently Uses a Truncated, 10-Digit Version of the ICN in Its Calls to HDR (Remedy 486375)

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Resolution: CPRS developers changed Remote Data Interoperability to use the full identifier rather than a truncated one.
texts.text: Resolution: CPRS developers changed Remote Data Interoperability to use the full identifier rather than a truncated one.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Note Label Disappearing (Remedy 486635)
texts.text: Note Label Disappearing (Remedy 486635)

texts.orig: – Previously, the line that displays how many notes have been entered for the patient disappeared when the user selected the New Note button.
texts.text: – Previously, the line that displays how many notes have been entered for the patient disappeared when the user selected the New Note button.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Resolution: CPRS developers corrected this problem. The number of notes for the patient will display above the Notes tree view display.
texts.text: Resolution: CPRS developers corrected this problem. The number of notes for the patient will display above the Notes tree view display.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Cannot Edit Notes for Reminder Dialogs (Remedy 486696)
texts.text: Cannot Edit Notes for Reminder Dialogs (Remedy 486696)

texts.orig: – Previously, users were unable to edit notes for reminder dialogs using the template editor. When importing a Clinical Reminder Dialog Template into Shared Templates the Show Template Notes did not display.
texts.text: – Previously, users were unable to edit notes for reminder dialogs using the template editor. When importing a Clinical Reminder Dialog Template into Shared Templates the Show Template Notes did not display.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Resolution: CPRS developers corrected this problem.
texts.text: Resolution: CPRS developers corrected this problem.

texts.text: CPRSUpdate.exe does not work in Windows 7 (540049)

texts.orig: Resolution: CPRS has changed CPRSUpdate.exe to work in the Windows 7 environment.
texts.text: Resolution: CPRS has changed CPRSUpdate.exe to work in the Windows 7 environment.

texts.text: Windows 7, MS Office 2010 Spell Check Not Working (Remedy 608146)

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Resolution: CPRS developers corrected the problem and the spell check should function correctly.
texts.text: Resolution: CPRS developers corrected the problem and the spell check should function correctly.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: CPRS Help Files Can’t Be Viewed in Window 7 Client (Remedy 622789)
texts.text: CPRS Help Files Can’t Be Viewed in Window 7 Client (Remedy 622789)

texts.label: text
texts.orig: — With the installation of Windows 7 and its accompanying changes, the help files that used the .hlp format will no longer be viewable unless the user downloads a fix from Microsoft.
texts.text: — With the installation of Windows 7 and its accompanying changes, the help files that used the .hlp format will no longer be viewable unless the user downloads a fix from Microsoft.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Resolution: CPRS now uses Help in a Hypertext Markup Language (HTML) format that will work with Windows 7.
texts.text: Resolution: CPRS now uses Help in a Hypertext Markup Language (HTML) format that will work with Windows 7.
